title: podman commit HTTP appends tag even if tag already set causing 500
body:
### Issue Description

Given the following request:
```
[19:15:58.511] DEBUG Caching container f18c67dc253b0f495d9daf7a2a0df7061c96fefbeed41de637d3d5beaa9139a7 as A/prettier:latest tool=prettier
  modem Sending: {
  path: '/commit?repo=A%2Fprettier%3Alatest&container=f18c67dc253b0f495d9daf7a2a0df7061c96fefbeed41de637d3d5beaa9139a7',
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Content-Length': 136 },
  key: undefined,
  cert: undefined,
  ca: undefined,
  socketPath: '/var/run/docker.sock'
} +1ms
```

On server side `A/prettier:latest` is normalized to `A/prettier:latest:latest` causing HTTP 500:
```
  modem Received: {"cause":"normalizing name for compat API: parsing reference \"A/prettier:latest:latest\": invalid reference format","message":"normalizing image: normalizing name for compat API: parsing reference \"A/prettier:latest:latest\": invalid reference format","response":500}
 +2ms
[19:15:58.513] ERROR Unknown error thrown during image run Error: (HTTP code 500) server error - normalizing image: normalizing name for compat API: parsing reference "A/prettier:latest:latest": invalid reference format  tool=prettier
  modem Sending: {
  path: '/containers/f18c67dc253b0f495d9daf7a2a0df7061c96fefbeed41de637d3d5beaa9139a7?force=true',
  method: 'DELETE',
  headers: {},
  key: undefined,
  cert: undefined,
  ca: undefined,
  socketPath: '/var/run/docker.sock'
} +0ms
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Send `/commit?repo=A%2Fprettier%3Alatest&container=f18c67dc253b0f495d9daf7a2a0df7061c96fefbeed41de637d3d5beaa9139a7` request.
2. Note failing response.

### Describe the results you received

500 error.

### Describe the results you expected

To succeed, note that removing the `:latest` name works:

```
  modem Sending: {
  path: '/commit?repo=A%2Fprettier&container=c9aa865ba0835ca5858ddc4374ad5096657ea9a2ab83559e3dd130c3479799be',
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Content-Length': 129 },
  key: undefined,
  cert: undefined,
  ca: undefined,
  socketPath: '/var/run/docker.sock'
} +2ms
  modem Received: {"Id":"8e3a537f572e0de5fca1c855169e8f54a729f3b84ccf94bf89950b3fd028ec6e"}
```

But obviously that now is not compatible with what docker does, or allowing custom tags to be commited.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

macOS.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Ah, this seems docker API is more permissive than should, as https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version/v1.49/#tag/Image/operation/ImageCommit states that `tag` should be set separately.
